name: opc-develop
description: Human-approved black-box semantics, executable testcase manifests, trusted runtime evidence, and semantics-routed product delivery

interface.shortDescription: Approved testcase semantics and runner-derived delivery evidence
interface.longDescription: opc-develop separates product truth from test code. Standard and releasable increments follow demo, PRD, testcase, then build: the human first approves an experienceable prototype, durable ACs, and exact black-box cases; testcase compiles a deterministic manifest; build implements only those approved cases. Project runners use Playwright as the primary deterministic UI driver, limit Computer Use to an atomic fallback, pre-arm success and failure observers, and emit structured evidence whose assembly, data, provider, driver, observation, and product-outcome axes determine authenticity. CLI flags cannot upgrade a result. Vibe remains explicitly unverified; lite handles localized changes without new E2E semantics, and copy/static-style/docs changes do not run E2E by default. Routing never uses predicted duration. Architect remains conditional, and ship/deploy/oncall/harness/retro close the engineering loop.
defaultPrompt: Use $opc-develop:vibe to implement this immediately without tests; I will perform the acceptance.
Use $opc-develop:lite to handle this small request on the current branch with targeted tests and before/after evidence.
Use $opc-develop:testcase after the approved demo and PRD to show me, review, and compile the exact executable black-box cases.
Use $opc-develop:build after testcase approval to implement the approved Core-Case with runner-derived evidence.
